contentBlocks.original:
महिम्नः पारं ते परमविदुषो यद्यसदृशी
स्तुतिर्ब्रह्मादीनामपि तदवसन्नास्त्वयि गिरः ।
अथाऽवाच्यः सर्वः स्वमतिपरिणामावधि गृणन्
ममाप्येष स्तोत्रे हर निरपवादः परिकरः ॥ १॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahimnaḥ pāraṃ te paramaviduṣo yadyasadṛśī
stutirbrahmādīnāmapi tadavasannāstvayi giraḥ .
athā'vācyaḥ sarvaḥ svamatipariṇāmāvadhi gṛṇan
mamāpyeṣa stotre hara nirapavādaḥ parikaraḥ .. 1..
contentBlocks.translation: O, Lord Shiva, remover of all types of miseries, what wonder is there, if the prayer to you, chanted by one who is ignorant about your greatness, is worthless! Because, even the utterance (speech) of Brahmaa and other gods is not able to fathom your merits (i.e., greatness). Hence, if persons with very limited intellect (and I am one of them) try to offer you a prayer, their attempt deserve your special favour. If it is so, I should not be an exception. Hence, (thinking like this) I begin this prayer. (1)
contentBlocks.meanings: महिम्नः (mahimnaḥ) – of greatness; पारं (pāraṃ) – limit; ते (te) – your; परमविदुषः (paramaviduṣaḥ) – of the supreme wise (parama:supreme | viduṣaḥ:of the wise); यत् (yat) – which; असदृशी (asadṛśī) – inadequate; स्तुतिः (stutiḥ) – praise; ब्रह्मादीनाम् (brahmādīnām) – of Brahma and others (brahma:Brahma | ādīnām:and others); अपि (api) – even; तत् (tat) – that; अवसन्नाः (avasannāḥ) – unable; त्वयि (tvayi) – in you; गिरः (giraḥ) – words; अथ (atha) – then; अवाच्यः (avācyaḥ) – ineffable; सर्वः (sarvaḥ) – all; स्वमतिपरिणामावधि (svamatipariṇāmāvadhi) – to the extent of one's intellect (sva:one's own | mati:intellect | pariṇāma:extent | avadhi:limit); गृणन् (gṛṇan) – praising; मम (mama) – my; अपि (api) – also; एषः (eṣaḥ) – this; स्तोत्रे (stotre) – in the hymn; हर (hara) – O Hara (Shiva); निरपवादः (nirapavādaḥ) – blameless; परिकरः (parikaraḥ) – support;

contentBlocks.original:
अतीतः पंथानं तव च महिमा वाङ्मनसयोः
अतद्व्यावृत्त्या यं चकितमभिधत्ते श्रुतिरपि ।
स कस्य स्तोतव्यः कतिविधगुणः कस्य विषयः
पदे त्वर्वाचीने पतति न मनः कस्य न वचः ॥ २॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atītaḥ paṃthānaṃ tava ca mahimā vāṅmanasayoḥ
atadvyāvṛttyā yaṃ cakitamabhidhatte śrutirapi .
sa kasya stotavyaḥ katividhaguṇaḥ kasya viṣayaḥ
pade tvarvācīne patati na manaḥ kasya na vacaḥ .. 2..
contentBlocks.translation: O, Great God, so great is your majesty that it cannot be reached by speech and mind. Even the Vedas also, having become surprised, confirm your greatness by only saying ‘Neti’, ‘Neti’ (not this, not this) while describing you. Who can praise this type of greatness of yours? With how many qualities is it composed? Whose subject of description can it be ? And yet even then whose mind and speech are not attached to your this new Saguna form ? (2)
contentBlocks.meanings: अतीतः (atītaḥ) – beyond; पंथानं (paṃthānam) – path; तव (tava) – your; च (ca) – and; महिमा (mahimā) – greatness; वाङ्मनसयोः (vāṅmanasayoḥ) – of speech and mind (vāk:speech | manasayoḥ:of mind); अतद्व्यावृत्त्या (atadvyāvṛttyā) – by negation (a:not | tad:that | vyāvṛttyā:by exclusion); यं (yaṃ) – whom; चकितम् (cakitam) – astonished; अभिधत्ते (abhidhatte) – describes; श्रुतिः (śrutiḥ) – Vedas; अपि (api) – also; स (sa) – he; कस्य (kasya) – whose; स्तोतव्यः (stotavyaḥ) – to be praised; कतिविधगुणः (katividhaguṇaḥ) – of how many qualities (kati:how many | vidha:types | guṇaḥ:qualities); कस्य (kasya) – whose; विषयः (viṣayaḥ) – subject; पदे (pade) – in the state; तु (tu) – but; अर्वाचीने (arvācīne) – in the present; पतति (patati) – falls; न (na) – not; मनः (manaḥ) – mind; कस्य (kasya) – whose; न (na) – not; वचः (vacaḥ) – speech;

contentBlocks.original:
मधुस्फीता वाचः परमममृतं निर्मितवतः
तव ब्रह्मन् किं वागपि सुरगुरोर्विस्मयपदम् ।
मम त्वेतां वाणीं गुणकथनपुण्येन भवतः
पुनामीत्यर्थेऽस्मिन् पुरमथन बुद्धिर्व्यवसिता ॥ ३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
madhusphītā vācaḥ paramamamṛtaṃ nirmitavataḥ
tava brahman kiṃ vāgapi suragurorvismayapadam .
mama tvetāṃ vāṇīṃ guṇakathanapuṇyena bhavataḥ
punāmītyarthe'smin puramathana buddhirvyavasitā .. 3..
contentBlocks.translation: O, Paramaatmaa (Greatest Soul), as you are the very creator of speech of the Vedas, which is like highest type of nectar and as sweet as honey, how can even the speech of Brahaspati (Guru, or spiritual guide of gods) surprise you ? (i.e., the speech of even Brahaspati is worthless before you). O, Destroyer of Three Cities of the demons, thinking that my speech may become purified by this act, my intellect (Buddhi) has become prepared to sing your greatness. (3)
contentBlocks.meanings: मधुस्फीता (madhusphītā) – sweet as honey (madhu:honey | sphītā:swollen, abundant); वाचः (vācaḥ) – speech; परमममृतं (paramamamṛtaṃ) – highest nectar (parama:highest | amṛtaṃ:nectar); निर्मितवतः (nirmitavataḥ) – created by; तव (tava) – your; ब्रह्मन् (brahman) – O Brahman; किं (kiṃ) – what; वाक् (vāk) – speech; अपि (api) – even; सुरगुरोः (suraguroḥ) – of the guru of gods (sura:gods | guroḥ:of the guru); विस्मयपदम् (vismayapadam) – cause of wonder (vismaya:wonder | padam:cause); मम (mama) – my; तु (tu) – but; एतां (etāṃ) – this; वाणीं (vāṇīṃ) – speech; गुणकथनपुण्येन (guṇakathanapuṇyena) – by virtue of praising qualities (guṇa:qualities | kathana:praising | puṇyena:by virtue of); भवतः (bhavataḥ) – of you; पुनामी (punāmī) – purify; इति (iti) – thus; अर्थे (arthe) – for the purpose; अस्मिन् (asmin) – in this; पुरमथन (puramathana) – destroyer of cities (pura:cities | mathana:destroyer); बुद्धिः (buddhiḥ) – intellect; व्यवसिता (vyavasitā) – determined;

contentBlocks.original:
तवैश्वर्यं यत्तज्जगदुदयरक्षाप्रलयकृत्
त्रयीवस्तु व्यस्तं तिस्रुषु गुणभिन्नासु तनुषु ।
अभव्यानामस्मिन् वरद रमणीयामरमणीं
विहन्तुं व्याक्रोशीं विदधत इहैके जडधियः ॥ ४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tavaiśvaryaṃ yattajjagadudayarakṣāpralayakṛt
trayīvastu vyastaṃ tisruṣu guṇabhinnāsu tanuṣu .
abhavyānāmasmin varada ramaṇīyāmaramaṇīṃ
vihantuṃ vyākrośīṃ vidadhata ihaike jaḍadhiyaḥ .. 4..
contentBlocks.translation: O, Giver of Boons, your greatness is the cause of creation, maintenance, and destruction of the whole universe; this is supported by three Vedas (i.e., Rigveda, Yajurveda, and Saamaveda); it is distributed in the three qualities (i.e., Satva, Rajas and Tamas) and three bodies (of Brahmaa, VishhNu and Mahesha). Such is your greatness but certain stupid persons in this world are trying to destroy it by slander, which may be delightful to them but is really undelightful. (4)
contentBlocks.meanings: तवैश्वर्यं (tavaiśvaryaṃ) – your greatness (tava:your | aiśvaryaṃ:greatness); यत् (yat) – which; तत् (tat) – that; जगत् (jagat) – universe; उदय (udaya) – creation; रक्षा (rakṣā) – maintenance; प्रलय (pralaya) – destruction; कृत् (kṛt) – cause; त्रयीवस्तु (trayīvastu) – supported by three Vedas (trayī:three Vedas | vastu:supported by); व्यस्तं (vyastaṃ) – distributed; तिस्रुषु (tisruṣu) – in the three; गुण (guṇa) – qualities; भिन्नासु (bhinnāsu) – divided; तनुषु (tanuṣu) – bodies; अभव्यानाम् (abhavyānām) – of the unworthy; अस्मिन् (asmin) – in this; वरद (varada) – O, Giver of Boons; रमणीयाम् (ramaṇīyām) – delightful; अरमणीं (aramaṇīṃ) – undelightful; विहन्तुं (vihantuṃ) – to destroy; व्याक्रोशीं (vyākrośīṃ) – slander; विदधत (vidadhata) – doing; इह (iha) – here; एके (eke) – some; जडधियः (jaḍadhiyaḥ) – stupid persons (jaḍa:stupid | dhiyaḥ:persons);

contentBlocks.original:
किमीहः किंकायः स खलु किमुपायस्त्रिभुवनं
किमाधारो धाता सृजति किमुपादान इति च ।
अतर्क्यैश्वर्ये त्वय्यनवसर दुःस्थो हतधियः
कुतर्कोऽयं कांश्चित् मुखरयति मोहाय जगतः ॥ ५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kimīhaḥ kiṃkāyaḥ sa khalu kimupāyastribhuvanaṃ
kimādhāro dhātā sṛjati kimupādāna iti ca .
atarkyaiśvarye tvayyanavasara duḥstho hatadhiyaḥ
kutarko'yaṃ kāṃścit mukharayati mohāya jagataḥ .. 5..
contentBlocks.translation: If the Paramaatmaa (the Greatest Soul) creates the three worlds (i.e., the whole Universe), what is his gesture ? What is his body ? What is his plan ? What is his basis (support)? What are his means (instruments, resources) ? These are the useless questions raised by some stupid critics, in order to mislead people, against one (i.e., you) who always remains incompatible to senses. (5)
contentBlocks.meanings: किम् (kim) – what; ईहः (īhaḥ) – effort; किम् (kim) – what; कायः (kāyaḥ) – body; स (sa) – he; खलु (khalu) – indeed; किम् (kim) – what; उपायः (upāyaḥ) – means; त्रिभुवनम् (tribhuvanam) – three worlds (tri:three | bhuvanam:worlds); किम् (kim) – what; आधारः (ādhāraḥ) – basis; धाता (dhātā) – creator; सृजति (sṛjati) – creates; किम् (kim) – what; उपादान (upādāna) – resources; इति (iti) – thus; च (ca) – and; अतर्क्य (atarkya) – incomprehensible; ऐश्वर्ये (aiśvarye) – in majesty; त्वयि (tvayi) – in you; अनवसर (anavasara) – without opportunity; दुःस्थः (duḥsthaḥ) – disturbed; हतधियः (hatadhiyaḥ) – of confused mind; कुतर्कः (kutarkaḥ) – false argument; अयम् (ayam) – this; कांश्चित् (kāṃścit) – some; मुखरयति (mukharayati) – makes vocal; मोहाय (mohāya) – for delusion; जगतः (jagataḥ) – of the world;

contentBlocks.original:
अजन्मानो लोकाः किमवयववन्तोऽपि जगतां
अधिष्ठातारं किं भवविधिरनादृत्य भवति ।
अनीशो वा कुर्याद् भुवनजनने कः परिकरो
यतो मन्दास्त्वां प्रत्यमरवर संशेरत इमे ॥ ६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ajanmāno lokāḥ kimavayavavanto'pi jagatāṃ
adhiṣṭhātāraṃ kiṃ bhavavidhiranādṛtya bhavati .
anīśo vā kuryād bhuvanajanane kaḥ parikaro
yato mandāstvāṃ pratyamaravara saṃśerata ime .. 6..
contentBlocks.translation: O, Best Of The Gods, are the seven Lokas (It is believed that there are seven worlds in this Universe, namely, Bhooloka, Bhuvarloka, Svargaloka, Maharloka, Janaloka, Tapaloka, and Satyaloka) unborn ? Was the birth of the Universe independent of its Lord (i.e., You) ? If it was so, then what were the means by which it was created that the stupid critics are creating doubts about you? (i.e., you are the only creator of the whole Universe). (6)
contentBlocks.meanings: अजन्मानः (ajanmānaḥ) – unborn; लोकाः (lokāḥ) – worlds; किम् (kim) – whether; अवयववन्तः (avayavavantaḥ) – having parts (avayava:parts | vantaḥ:having); अपि (api) – also; जगताम् (jagatām) – of the worlds; अधिष्ठातारम् (adhiṣṭhātāram) – ruler; किम् (kim) – whether; भवविधिः (bhavavidhiḥ) – creation process (bhava:existence | vidhiḥ:process); अनादृत्य (anādṛtya) – disregarding; भवति (bhavati) – becomes; अनीशः (anīśaḥ) – powerless; वा (vā) – or; कुर्यात् (kuryāt) – would do; भुवनजनने (bhuvanajanane) – in world creation (bhuvana:world | janane:creation); कः (kaḥ) – who; परिकरः (parikaraḥ) – means; यतः (yataḥ) – from which; मन्दाः (mandāḥ) – fools; त्वाम् (tvām) – you; प्रति (prati) – towards; अमरवर (amaravara) – best of the gods (amara:immortal | vara:best); संशेरते (saṃśerate) – doubt; इमे (ime) – these;

contentBlocks.original:
त्रयी साङ्ख्यं योगः पशुपतिमतं वैष्णवमिति
प्रभिन्ने प्रस्थाने परमिदमदः पथ्यमिति च ।
रुचीनां वैचित्र्यादृजुकुटिल नानापथजुषां
नृणामेको गम्यस्त्वमसि पयसामर्णव इव ॥ ७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
trayī sāṅkhyaṃ yogaḥ paśupatimataṃ vaiṣṇavamiti
prabhinne prasthāne paramidamadaḥ pathyamiti ca .
rucīnāṃ vaicitryādṛjukuṭila nānāpathajuṣāṃ
nṛṇāmeko gamyastvamasi payasāmarṇava iva .. 7..
contentBlocks.translation: The different practices based on the three Vedas, SaMkhya, Yoga, Pashupata- mata, VaishhNava-mata etc. are but different paths (to reach to the Greatest Truth) and people on account of their different aptitude choose from them whatever they think best and deserved to be accepted. But as the sea is the final resting place for all types of streams , You are the only reaching place for all people whichever path,straight or zigzag, they may accept. (7)
contentBlocks.meanings: त्रयी (trayī) – threefold; साङ्ख्यम् (sāṅkhyam) – Sankhya philosophy; योगः (yogaḥ) – Yoga; पशुपतिमतम् (paśupatimataṃ) – Pashupata doctrine (paśupati:Lord of creatures | matam:doctrine); वैष्णवम् (vaiṣṇavam) – Vaishnava doctrine; इति (iti) – thus; प्रभिन्ने (prabhinne) – divided; प्रस्थाने (prasthāne) – in paths; परम् (param) – supreme; इदम् (idam) – this; अदः (adaḥ) – that; पथ्यम् (pathyam) – suitable; इति (iti) – thus; च (ca) – and; रुचीनाम् (rucīnām) – of tastes; वैचित्र्यात् (vaicitryāt) – due to variety; ऋजुकुटिल (ṛjukuṭila) – straight and crooked (ṛju:straight | kuṭila:crooked); नानापथजुषाम् (nānāpathajuṣām) – of various path followers (nānā:various | patha:path | juṣām:followers); नृणाम् (nṛṇām) – of people; एकः (ekaḥ) – one; गम्यः (gamyaḥ) – destination; त्वम् (tvam) – you; असि (asi) – are; पयसाम् (payasām) – of waters; अर्णवः (arṇavaḥ) – ocean; इव (iva) – like;

contentBlocks.original:
महोक्षः खट्वाङ्गं परशुरजिनं भस्म फणिनः
कपालं चेतीयत्तव वरद तन्त्रोपकरणम् ।
सुरास्तां तामृद्धिं दधति तु भवद्भूप्रणिहितां
न हि स्वात्मारामं विषयमृगतृष्णा भ्रमयति ॥ ८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahokṣaḥ khaṭvāṅgaṃ paraśurajinaṃ bhasma phaṇinaḥ
kapālaṃ cetīyattava varada tantropakaraṇam .
surāstāṃ tāmṛddhiṃ dadhati tu bhavadbhūpraṇihitāṃ
na hi svātmārāmaṃ viṣayamṛgatṛṣṇā bhramayati .. 8..
contentBlocks.translation: O, Giver of the Boons, the bull, the parts of a cot, chisel, the elephant-skin, Ashes, the serpent, the skull : these are the articles of your house-hold. And yet gods get all their riches merely by the movement of your eye-brows. Really, false desires for worldly things do not deceive (mislead) one who is always is absorbed in his soul (i.e., the Yogi-in fact You). (8)
contentBlocks.meanings: महोक्षः (mahokṣaḥ) – the bull; खट्वाङ्गं (khaṭvāṅgaṃ) – parts of a cot; परशु (paraśu) – chisel; अजिनं (ajinaṃ) – elephant-skin; भस्म (bhasma) – ashes; फणिनः (phaṇinaḥ) – of the serpent; कपालं (kapālaṃ) – the skull; च (ca) – and; इति (iti) – thus; यत् (yat) – which; तव (tava) – your; वरद (varada) – Giver of the Boons; तन्त्र (tantra) – articles; उपकरणम् (upakaraṇam) – household; सुराः (surāḥ) – gods; ताम् (tām) – that; ताम् (tām) – that; ऋद्धिं (ṛddhiṃ) – riches; दधति (dadhati) – get; तु (tu) – but; भवत् (bhavat) – your; भू (bhū) – earth; प्रणिहितां (praṇihitāṃ) – by movement; न (na) – not; हि (hi) – indeed; स्वात्मारामं (svātmārāmaṃ) – absorbed in his soul (sva:own | ātma:soul | ārāmaṃ:absorbed); विषय (viṣaya) – worldly things; मृगतृष्णा (mṛgatṛṣṇā) – false desires; भ्रमयति (bhramayati) – mislead;

contentBlocks.original:
ध्रुवं कश्चित् सर्वं सकलमपरस्त्वध्रुवमिदं
परो ध्रौव्याऽध्रौव्ये जगति गदति व्यस्तविषये ।
समस्तेऽप्येतस्मिन् पुरमथन तैर्विस्मित इव
स्तुवन् जिह्रेमि त्वां न खलु ननु धृष्टा मुखरता ॥ ९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhruvaṃ kaścit sarvaṃ sakalamaparastvadhruvamidaṃ
paro dhrauvyā'dhrauvye jagati gadati vyastaviṣaye .
samaste'pyetasmin puramathana tairvismita iva
stuvan jihremi tvāṃ na khalu nanu dhṛṣṭā mukharatā .. 9..
contentBlocks.translation: O, Destroyer Of (Three) Cities, some persons call this Universe eternal (ever lasting), others call it temporary, and yet others call it both eternal and temporary. Hence, being surprised (perplexed) by these contradictory opinions on this subject, I am really becoming immodest in loquaciously praising You. (9)
contentBlocks.meanings: ध्रुवं (dhruvam) – eternal; कश्चित् (kaścit) – some; सर्वं (sarvam) – all; सकलम् (sakalam) – entire; अपरः (aparaḥ) – another; तु (tu) – but; अध्रुवम् (adhruvam) – temporary; इदं (idaṃ) – this; परः (paraḥ) – another; ध्रौव्ये (dhrauvye) – eternal; अध्रौव्ये (adhrauvye) – temporary; जगति (jagati) – in the world; गदति (gadati) – speaks; व्यस्तविषये (vyastaviṣaye) – in contradictory subjects (vyasta:contradictory | viṣaye:in subjects); समस्ते (samaste) – in all; अपि (api) – even; एतस्मिन् (etasmin) – in this; पुरमथन (puramathana) – Destroyer of Cities (pura:cities | mathana:destroyer); तैः (taiḥ) – by them; विस्मित (vismita) – astonished; इव (iva) – as if; स्तुवन् (stuvan) – praising; जिह्रेमि (jihremi) – I am ashamed; त्वां (tvāṃ) – you; न (na) – not; खलु (khalu) – indeed; ननु (nanu) – certainly; धृष्टा (dhṛṣṭā) – bold; मुखरता (mukharatā) – loquacity;

contentBlocks.original:
तवैश्वर्यं यत्नाद् यदुपरि विरिञ्चिर्हरिरधः
परिच्छेतुं यातावनिलमनलस्कन्धवपुषः ।
ततो भक्तिश्रद्धा-भरगुरु-गृणद्भ्यां गिरिश यत्
स्वयं तस्थे ताभ्यां तव किमनुवृत्तिर्न फलति ॥ १०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tavaiśvaryaṃ yatnād yadupari viriñcirhariradhaḥ
paricchetuṃ yātāvanilamanalaskandhavapuṣaḥ .
tato bhaktiśraddhā-bharaguru-gṛṇadbhyāṃ giriśa yat
svayaṃ tasthe tābhyāṃ tava kimanuvṛttirna phalati .. 10..
contentBlocks.translation: Brahma and VishhNu wanted to measure your wealth i.e. greatness. You took the form of Fire and your whole body was a column of fire extending over space. While Brahma took the form of a swan and flew high to see the top (head), VishhNu took the form of a boar and dug up downwards to see the bottom (feet). Neither could succeed. (While VishhNu confessed the truth, Brahma falsely claimed that he had found the top and persuaded the Ketaki flower to bear false witness. Shiva punished Brahma by removing one of his 5 heads and ordered that henceforth the Ketaki flower should not be used for his worship).When ultimately both praised you with full devotion and faith, you stood before them revealing your normal form. O, mountain-dweller, does not toeing your line always bear fruit? (10)
contentBlocks.meanings: तवैश्वर्यं (tavaiśvaryam) – your greatness (tava:your | aiśvaryam:greatness); यत्नात् (yatnāt) – by effort; यदुपरि (yadupari) – above which (yat:which | upari:above); विरिञ्चिः (viriñciḥ) – Brahma; हरिः (hariḥ) – Vishnu; अधः (adhaḥ) – below; परिच्छेतुं (paricchetuṃ) – to measure; यातौ (yātau) – went; अनिल (anila) – wind; अनल (anala) – fire; स्कन्धवपुषः (skandhavapuṣaḥ) – having a body like a column (skandha:column | vapuṣaḥ:of body); ततः (tataḥ) – then; भक्तिश्रद्धा-भरगुरु-गृणद्भ्यां (bhaktiśraddhā-bharaguru-gṛṇadbhyām) – by those filled with devotion and faith (bhakti:devotion | śraddhā:faith | bhara:filled with | guru:heavy | gṛṇadbhyām:by those praising); गिरिश (giriśa) – O mountain-dweller; यत् (yat) – which; स्वयं (svayam) – yourself; तस्थे (tasthe) – stood; ताभ्यां (tābhyām) – by them; तव (tava) – your; किमनुवृत्तिः (kimanuvṛttiḥ) – does not following (kim:does not | anuvṛttiḥ:following); न (na) – not; फलति (phalati) – bear fruit;

contentBlocks.original:
अयत्नादासाद्य त्रिभुवनमवैरव्यतिकरं
दशास्यो यद्बाहूनभृत-रणकण्डू-परवशान् ।
शिरःपद्मश्रेणी-रचितचरणाम्भोरुह-बलेः
स्थिरायास्त्वद्भक्तेस्त्रिपुरहर विस्फूर्जितमिदम् ॥ ११॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ayatnādāsādya tribhuvanamavairavyatikaraṃ
daśāsyo yadbāhūnabhṛta-raṇakaṇḍū-paravaśān .
śiraḥpadmaśreṇī-racitacaraṇāmbhoruha-baleḥ
sthirāyāstvadbhaktestripurahara visphūrjitamidam .. 11..
contentBlocks.translation: Oh,destroyer of the three cities! The effortless achievement of the ten-headed Ravana in making the three worlds enemyless (having conquered) and his arrant eagerness for further fight by stretching his arms, are but the result of his constant devotion to your lotus feet at which he ever laid the lotus garland consisting of his 10 heads! (11)
contentBlocks.meanings: अयत्नात् (ayatnāt) – effortlessly; आसाद्य (āsādya) – having achieved; त्रिभुवनम् (tribhuvanam) – three worlds (tri:three | bhuvanam:worlds); अवैरव्यतिकरम् (avairavyatikaram) – enemyless (avaira:without enemies | vyatikaram:conflict); दशास्यः (daśāsyaḥ) – ten-headed (daśa:ten | āsyaḥ:headed); यत् (yat) – whose; बाहून् (bāhūn) – arms; अभृत (abhṛta) – bearing; रणकण्डू (raṇakaṇḍū) – itch for battle (raṇa:battle | kaṇḍū:itch); परवशान् (paravaśān) – subdued; शिरःपद्मश्रेणी (śiraḥpadmaśreṇī) – garland of lotus heads (śiraḥ:heads | padma:lotus | śreṇī:garland); रचित (racita) – made; चरणाम्भोरुह (caraṇāmbhoruha) – lotus feet (caraṇa:feet | ambhoruha:lotus); बलेः (baleḥ) – strength; स्थिरायाः (sthirāyāḥ) – steadfast; त्वद्भक्तेः (tvadbhakteḥ) – your devotion (tvad:your | bhakteḥ:devotion); त्रिपुरहर (tripurahara) – destroyer of three cities (tri:three | pura:cities | hara:destroyer); विस्फूर्जितम् (visphūrjitam) – manifestation; इदम् (idam) – this;

contentBlocks.original:
अमुष्य त्वत्सेवा-समधिगतसारं भुजवनं
बलात् कैलासेऽपि त्वदधिवसतौ विक्रमयतः ।
अलभ्यापातालेऽप्यलसचलितांगुष्ठशिरसि
प्रतिष्ठा त्वय्यासीद् ध्रुवमुपचितो मुह्यति खलः ॥ १२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
amuṣya tvatsevā-samadhigatasāraṃ bhujavanaṃ
balāt kailāse'pi tvadadhivasatau vikramayataḥ .
alabhyāpātāle'pyalasacalitāṃguṣṭhaśirasi
pratiṣṭhā tvayyāsīd dhruvamupacito muhyati khalaḥ .. 12..
contentBlocks.translation: Having obtained all his prowess through worshipping you, RavaNa once dared to test the power of his arms at your own dwelling place (Kailas Mountain). When he tried to lift it up, you just moved a toe of your foot on a head of his and lo! Ravana could not find rest or peace even in the nether-world. Surely, power maddens the wicked. Finally RavaNa reestablished his faith in you. (12)
contentBlocks.meanings: अमुष्य (amuṣya) – of him; त्वत्सेवा-समधिगतसारं (tvatsevā-samadhigatasāraṃ) – obtained essence through serving you (tvat:your | sevā:service | samadhigata:obtained | sāraṃ:essence); भुजवनं (bhujavanaṃ) – strength of arms (bhuja:arm | vanaṃ:forest/strength); बलात् (balāt) – by force; कैलासेऽपि (kailāse'pi) – even at Kailasa (kailāse:at Kailasa | api:even); त्वदधिवसतौ (tvadadhivasatau) – at your dwelling (tvad:your | adhivasatau:dwelling); विक्रमयतः (vikramayataḥ) – of the one exerting; अलभ्यापातालेऽपि (alabhyāpātāle'pi) – even in unreachable nether-world (alabhya:unreachable | pātāle:in nether-world | api:even); अलसचलितांगुष्ठशिरसि (alasacalitāṃguṣṭhaśirasi) – on the moved toe head (alasa:moved | calita:moved | aṅguṣṭha:toe | śirasi:on the head); प्रतिष्ठा (pratiṣṭhā) – established; त्वय्यासीद् (tvayyāsīd) – was in you (tvayi:in you | āsīt:was); ध्रुवम् (dhruvam) – surely; उपचितो (upacito) – accumulated; मुह्यति (muhyati) – bewildered; खलः (khalaḥ) – wicked person;

contentBlocks.original:
यदृद्धिं सुत्राम्णो वरद परमोच्चैरपि सतीं
अधश्चक्रे बाणः परिजनविधेयत्रिभुवनः ।
न तच्चित्रं तस्मिन् वरिवसितरि त्वच्चरणयोः
न कस्याप्युन्नत्यै भवति शिरसस्त्वय्यवनतिः ॥ १३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yadṛddhiṃ sutrāmṇo varada paramoccairapi satīṃ
adhaścakre bāṇaḥ parijanavidheyatribhuvanaḥ .
na taccitraṃ tasmin varivasitari tvaccaraṇayoḥ
na kasyāpyunnatyai bhavati śirasastvayyavanatiḥ .. 13..
contentBlocks.translation: Oh boon-giver! BaaNa, the demon king made all the three worlds serve him with all their attendants and even the greatest wealth of Indra was a trifle for him. It was not a surprise at all, since he ‘dwelt’ in your feet; who does not rise in life by bowing his head to you? (13)
contentBlocks.meanings: यदृद्धिं (yadṛddhiṃ) – that prosperity; सुत्राम्णः (sutrāmṇaḥ) – of Indra; वरद (varada) – Oh boon-giver; परमोच्चैः (paramoccaiḥ) – extremely high; अपि (api) – even; सतीम् (satīm) – existing; अधःचक्रे (adhaścakre) – made lower; बाणः (bāṇaḥ) – BaaNa; परिजनविधेयत्रिभुवनः (parijanavidheyatribhuvanaḥ) – serving three worlds with attendants (parijana:attendants | vidheya:obedient | tribhuvanaḥ:three worlds); न (na) – not; तत् (tat) – that; चित्रम् (citram) – surprising; तस्मिन् (tasmin) – in him; वरिवसितरि (varivasitari) – dwelling; त्वच्चरणयोः (tvaccaraṇayoḥ) – in your feet (tvat:your | caraṇayoḥ:feet); न (na) – not; कस्यापि (kasyāpi) – of anyone; उन्नत्यै (unnatyai) – for elevation; भवति (bhavati) – becomes; शिरसः (śirasaḥ) – of the head; त्वयि (tvayi) – to you; अवनतिः (avanatiḥ) – bowing;

contentBlocks.original:
अकाण्ड-ब्रह्माण्ड-क्षयचकित-देवासुरकृपा
विधेयस्याऽऽसीद् यस्त्रिनयन विषं संहृतवतः ।
स कल्माषः कण्ठे तव न कुरुते न श्रियमहो
विकारोऽपि श्लाघ्यो भुवन-भय- भङ्ग- व्यसनिनः ॥ १४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
akāṇḍa-brahmāṇḍa-kṣayacakita-devāsurakṛpā
vidheyasyā''sīd yastrinayana viṣaṃ saṃhṛtavataḥ .
sa kalmāṣaḥ kaṇṭhe tava na kurute na śriyamaho
vikāro'pi ślāghyo bhuvana-bhaya- bhaṅga- vyasaninaḥ .. 14..
contentBlocks.translation: When the ocean was being churned by the gods and demons for ‘amRit.h’ (nectar), various objects came forth: at one point, there emerged the ‘kAlakUTa’ poison which threatened to consume everything. The gods as well as the demons were stunned at the prospect of the entire universe coming to an end, O, three-eyed lord, who is ever compassionate and engaged in removing the fear of the world, you took it (poison) on yourself by consuming it. (On Parvati’s holding Shiva’s throat at that point, the poison froze blue there itself and Shiva became ‘neelakanTha’). It is strange that this stain in your neck, though appearing to be a deformity, actually adds to your richness and personality. (14)
contentBlocks.meanings: अकाण्ड (akāṇḍa) – uninterrupted; ब्रह्माण्ड (brahmāṇḍa) – universe; क्षयचकित (kṣayacakita) – fearful of destruction (kṣaya:destruction | cakita:fearful); देवासुरकृपा (devāsurakṛpā) – compassion for gods and demons (deva:gods | asura:demons | kṛpā:compassion); विधेयस्य (vidheyasya) – of the obedient; आसीत् (āsīt) – was; यः (yaḥ) – who; त्रिनयन (trinayana) – three-eyed (tri:three | nayana:eye); विषं (viṣaṃ) – poison; संहृतवतः (saṃhṛtavataḥ) – who consumed; स (sa) – that; कल्माषः (kalmāṣaḥ) – stain; कण्ठे (kaṇṭhe) – in the throat; तव (tava) – your; न (na) – not; कुरुते (kurute) – does; न (na) – not; श्रियम् (śriyam) – beauty; अहो (aho) – indeed; विकारः (vikāraḥ) – deformity; अपि (api) – even; श्लाघ्यः (ślāghyaḥ) – praiseworthy; भुवन (bhuvana) – world; भय (bhaya) – fear; भङ्ग (bhaṅga) – removal; व्यसनिनः (vyasaninaḥ) – engaged in;

contentBlocks.original:
असिद्धार्था नैव क्वचिदपि सदेवासुरनरे
निवर्तन्ते नित्यं जगति जयिनो यस्य विशिखाः ।
स पश्यन्नीश त्वामितरसुरसाधारणमभूत्
स्मरः स्मर्तव्यात्मा न हि वशिषु पथ्यः परिभवः ॥ १५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
asiddhārthā naiva kvacidapi sadevāsuranare
nivartante nityaṃ jagati jayino yasya viśikhāḥ .
sa paśyannīśa tvāmitarasurasādhāraṇamabhūt
smaraḥ smartavyātmā na hi vaśiṣu pathyaḥ paribhavaḥ .. 15..
contentBlocks.translation: The cupid’s (love-god ‘manmatha’s) (flower) arrows never return unaccomplished whether the victims were gods or demons or men. However O, master! he has now become just a remembered soul (without body), since he looked upon you as any other ordinary god, shot his arrow and got burnt to ashes,in no time. Insulting, masters (who have controlled their senses), does one no good. (15)
contentBlocks.meanings: असिद्धार्थाः (asiddhārthāḥ) – unaccomplished; न (na) – not; एव (eva) – indeed; क्वचित् (kvacit) – anywhere; अपि (api) – even; सदेव-असुर-नरे (sadeva-asura-nare) – with gods, demons, men (sadeva:with gods | asura:demons | nare:men); निवर्तन्ते (nivartante) – return; नित्यं (nityaṃ) – always; जगति (jagati) – in the world; जयिनः (jayinaḥ) – victorious; यस्मिन् (yasmin) – whose; विशिखाः (viśikhāḥ) – arrows; सः (saḥ) – he; पश्यन् (paśyan) – seeing; ईश (īśa) – O Lord; त्वाम् (tvām) – you; इतर-सुर-साधारणम् (itara-sura-sādhāraṇam) – ordinary among other gods (itara:other | sura:gods | sādhāraṇam:ordinary); अभूत् (abhūt) – became; स्मरः (smaraḥ) – Cupid; स्मर्तव्य-आत्मा (smartavya-ātmā) – remembered soul (smartavya:remembered | ātmā:soul); न (na) – not; हि (hi) – indeed; वशिषु (vaśiṣu) – among the controlled; पथ्यः (pathyaḥ) – beneficial; परिभवः (paribhavaḥ) – insult;

contentBlocks.original:
मही पादाघाताद् व्रजति सहसा संशयपदं
पदं विष्णोर्भ्राम्यद् भुज-परिघ-रुग्ण-ग्रह- गणम् ।
मुहुर्द्यौर्दौस्थ्यं यात्यनिभृत-जटा-ताडित-तटा
जगद्रक्षायै त्वं नटसि ननु वामैव विभुता ॥ १६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahī pādāghātād vrajati sahasā saṃśayapadaṃ
padaṃ viṣṇorbhrāmyad bhuja-parigha-rugṇa-graha- gaṇam .
muhurdyaurdausthyaṃ yātyanibhṛta-jaṭā-tāḍita-taṭā
jagadrakṣāyai tvaṃ naṭasi nanu vāmaiva vibhutā .. 16..
contentBlocks.translation: You dance for protecting the world, but strangely, your glorious act appears to produce the opposite result in that the earth suddenly struck by your dancing feet doubts that it is coming to an end; even VishhNu’s domain is shaken in fear when your mace like arms bruise the planets; the godly region feels miserable when its banks are struck by your agitated matted locks (of hair)! (16)
contentBlocks.meanings: मही (mahī) – earth; पादाघातात् (pādāghātāt) – from the strike of feet (pāda:feet | āghātāt:from the strike); व्रजति (vrajati) – goes; सहसा (sahasā) – suddenly; संशयपदं (saṃśayapadaṃ) – state of doubt (saṃśaya:doubt | padam:state); पदं (padam) – state; विष्णोः (viṣṇoḥ) – of Vishnu; भ्राम्यद् (bhrāmyad) – whirling; भुज-परिघ-रुग्ण-ग्रह-गणम् (bhuja-parigha-rugṇa-graha-gaṇam) – group of planets struck by arms (bhuja:arms | parigha:mace-like | rugṇa:struck | graha:planets | gaṇam:group); मुहुः (muhuḥ) – repeatedly; द्यौः (dyauḥ) – heaven; दौस्थ्यं (dausthyaṃ) – misery; याति (yāti) – goes; अनिभृत-जटा-ताडित-तटा (anibhṛta-jaṭā-tāḍita-taṭā) – banks struck by matted locks (anibhṛta:agitated | jaṭā:matted locks | tāḍita:struck | taṭā:banks); जगद्रक्षायै (jagadrakṣāyai) – for protecting the world (jagat:world | rakṣāyai:for protecting); त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; नटसि (naṭasi) – dance; ननु (nanu) – indeed; वामैव (vāmaiva) – strangely; विभुता (vibhutā) – glorious act;

contentBlocks.original:
वियद्व्यापी तारा-गण-गुणित-फेनोद्गम-रुचिः
प्रवाहो वारां यः पृषतलघुदृष्टः शिरसि ते ।
जगद्द्वीपाकारं जलधिवलयं तेन कृतमिति
अनेनैवोन्नेयं धृतमहिम दिव्यं तव वपुः ॥ १७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viyadvyāpī tārā-gaṇa-guṇita-phenodgama-ruciḥ
pravāho vārāṃ yaḥ pṛṣatalaghudṛṣṭaḥ śirasi te .
jagaddvīpākāraṃ jaladhivalayaṃ tena kṛtamiti
anenaivonneyaṃ dhṛtamahima divyaṃ tava vapuḥ .. 17..
contentBlocks.translation: The divine river flows extensively through the sky and its charm is enhanced by the illumination of the foam by the groups of stars. (Brought down to the earth by the King Bhagiratha by propitiating Lord Shiva and known as Ganga) it creates many islands and whirlpools on the earth. The same turbulent river appears like a mere droplet of water on your head. This itself shows how lofty and divine your body (form) is! (17)
contentBlocks.meanings: वियद्व्यापी (viyadvyāpī) – pervading the sky (viyat:sky | vyāpī:pervading); तारा-गण-गुणित-फेनोद्गम-रुचिः (tārā-gaṇa-guṇita-phenodgama-ruciḥ) – charm enhanced by star groups (tārā:stars | gaṇa:group | guṇita:enhanced | phena:foam | udgama:emergence | ruciḥ:charm); प्रवाहः (pravāhaḥ) – flow; वारां (vārāṃ) – of waters; यः (yaḥ) – which; पृषतलघुदृष्टः (pṛṣatalaghudṛṣṭaḥ) – seen as a droplet (pṛṣata:droplet | laghu:small | dṛṣṭaḥ:seen); शिरसि (śirasi) – on the head; ते (te) – your; जगद्द्वीपाकारं (jagaddvīpākāraṃ) – world-island form (jagat:world | dvīpa:island | ākāraṃ:form); जलधिवलयं (jaladhivalayaṃ) – ocean circle (jaladhi:ocean | valayaṃ:circle); तेन (tena) – by that; कृतमिति (kṛtamiti) – is made (kṛtam:made | iti:thus); अनेनैवोन्नेयं (anenaivonneyaṃ) – by this itself inferred (anena:by this | eva:itself | unneyam:inferred); धृतमहिम (dhṛtamahima) – bearing greatness (dhṛta:bearing | mahima:greatness); दिव्यं (divyaṃ) – divine; तव (tava) – your; वपुः (vapuḥ) – form;

contentBlocks.original:
रथः क्षोणी यन्ता शतधृतिरगेन्द्रो धनुरथो
रथाङ्गे चन्द्रार्कौ रथ-चरण-पाणिः शर इति ।
दिधक्षोस्ते कोऽयं त्रिपुरतृणमाडम्बर विधिः
विधेयैः क्रीडन्त्यो न खलु परतन्त्राः प्रभुधियः ॥ १८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rathaḥ kṣoṇī yantā śatadhṛtiragendro dhanuratho
rathāṅge candrārkau ratha-caraṇa-pāṇiḥ śara iti .
didhakṣoste ko'yaṃ tripuratṛṇamāḍambara vidhiḥ
vidheyaiḥ krīḍantyo na khalu paratantrāḥ prabhudhiyaḥ .. 18..
contentBlocks.translation: When you wanted to burn the three cities, you had the earth as the chariot, Brahma as the charioteer, the Meru mountain as the bow, the sun and the moon as the parts of the chariot and VishhNu himself (who holds the chariot-wheel in his hand - Sudarshan chakra), as the arrow. Why this demonstrative show when you as the dictator of everything, could have done the job as a trifle? The Lord’s greatness is not dependent on anybody or anything. (Incidentally there is a view that the burning of the three cities would refer to the burning of three kinds of bodies of man i.e., ‘sthUla sharIra’, ‘sUkshma sharIra’ and ‘kAraNa sharIra’). (18)
contentBlocks.meanings: रथः (rathaḥ) – chariot; क्षोणी (kṣoṇī) – earth; यन्ता (yantā) – charioteer; शतधृतिः (śatadhṛtiḥ) – Brahma; अगेन्द्रः (agendraḥ) – Meru mountain; धनुः (dhanuḥ) – bow; अथ (atha) – then; रथाङ्गे (rathāṅge) – parts of the chariot (ratha:chariot | aṅge:parts); चन्द्रार्कौ (candrārkau) – moon and sun (candra:moon | arka:sun); रथ-चरण-पाणिः (ratha-caraṇa-pāṇiḥ) – chariot-wheel holder (ratha:chariot | caraṇa:wheel | pāṇiḥ:holder); शरः (śaraḥ) – arrow; इति (iti) – thus; दिधक्षोः (didhakṣoḥ) – desiring to burn; ते (te) – your; कः (kaḥ) – what; अयम् (ayam) – this; त्रिपुरतृणम् (tripuratṛṇam) – three cities as grass (tripura:three cities | tṛṇam:grass); आडम्बर (āḍambara) – demonstrative show; विधिः (vidhiḥ) – method; विधेयैः (vidheyaiḥ) – by subordinates; क्रीडन्त्यः (krīḍantyaḥ) – playing; न (na) – not; खलु (khalu) – indeed; परतन्त्राः (paratantrāḥ) – dependent; प्रभुधियः (prabhudhiyaḥ) – minds of the lord (prabhu:lord | dhiyaḥ:minds);

contentBlocks.original:
हरिस्ते साहस्रं कमल बलिमाधाय पदयोः
यदेकोने तस्मिन् निजमुदहरन्नेत्रकमलम् ।
गतो भक्त्युद्रेकः परिणतिमसौ चक्रवपुषः
त्रयाणां रक्षायै त्रिपुरहर जागर्ति जगताम् ॥ १९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hariste sāhasraṃ kamala balimādhāya padayoḥ
yadekone tasmin nijamudaharannetrakamalam .
gato bhaktyudrekaḥ pariṇatimasau cakravapuṣaḥ
trayāṇāṃ rakṣāyai tripurahara jāgarti jagatām .. 19..
contentBlocks.translation: VishhNu once brought 1000 lotuses and was placing them at your feet; after placing 999 flowers he found that one was missing; he plucked out one of his own eyes and offered it as a lotus; this supreme exemplification of devotion on his part was transformed into the wheel (sudarshana chakra) in his hand, which he uses for protecting the world. (19)
contentBlocks.meanings: हरिः (hariḥ) – Vishnu; ते (te) – your; साहस्रं (sāhasraṃ) – thousand; कमल (kamala) – lotuses; बलिम् (balim) – offering; आधाय (ādhāya) – having placed; पदयोः (padayoḥ) – at the feet; यत् (yat) – which; एकोने (ekone) – one less; तस्मिन् (tasmin) – in that; निजम् (nijam) – own; उदहरन् (udaharan) – uttering; नेत्रकमलम् (netrakamalam) – eye as lotus (netra:eye | kamalam:lotus); गतः (gataḥ) – gone; भक्त्युद्रेकः (bhaktyudrekaḥ) – intense devotion (bhakti:devotion | udrekaḥ:intensity); परिणतिम् (pariṇatim) – transformation; असौ (asau) – this; चक्रवपुषः (cakravapuṣaḥ) – form of a wheel (cakra:wheel | vapuṣaḥ:form of); त्रयाणां (trayāṇāṃ) – of the three; रक्षायै (rakṣāyai) – for protection; त्रिपुरहर (tripurahara) – destroyer of Tripura (tri:three | pura:cities | hara:destroyer); जागर्ति (jāgarti) – awakens; जगताम् (jagatām) – of the worlds;

contentBlocks.original:
क्रतौ सुप्ते जाग्रत् त्वमसि फलयोगे क्रतुमतां
क्व कर्म प्रध्वस्तं फलति पुरुषाराधनमृते ।
अतस्त्वां सम्प्रेक्ष्य क्रतुषु फलदान-प्रतिभुवं
श्रुतौ श्रद्धां बध्वा दृढपरिकरः कर्मसु जनः ॥ २०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kratau supte jāgrat tvamasi phalayoge kratumatāṃ
kva karma pradhvastaṃ phalati puruṣārādhanamṛte .
atastvāṃ samprekṣya kratuṣu phaladāna-pratibhuvaṃ
śrutau śraddhāṃ badhvā dṛḍhaparikaraḥ karmasu janaḥ .. 20..
contentBlocks.translation: You ensure that there is a connection between cause and effect and hence when men perform a sacrifice they obtain good results. Otherwise how can there be future result for a past action? Thus on seeing your power in rewarding people performing sacrificial worship, with good results, men believe in Vedas and firmly engage themselves in various worshipful acts. (20)
contentBlocks.meanings: क्रतौ (kratau) – in the sacrifice; सुप्ते (supte) – asleep; जाग्रत् (jāgrat) – awake; त्वम् (tvam) – you; असि (asi) – are; फलयोगे (phalayoge) – in the connection with results (phala:result | yoge:in the connection); क्रतुमतां (kratumatām) – of the sacrificers (kratu:sacrifice | matām:of those having); क्व (kva) – where; कर्म (karma) – action; प्रध्वस्तं (pradhvastaṃ) – destroyed; फलति (phalati) – bears fruit; पुरुषाराधनमृते (puruṣārādhanamṛte) – without worship of the Supreme (puruṣa:Supreme Person | ārādhana:worship | mṛte:without); अतः (ataḥ) – therefore; त्वां (tvāṃ) – you; सम्प्रेक्ष्य (samprekṣya) – seeing; क्रतुषु (kratuṣu) – in sacrifices; फलदान-प्रतिभुवं (phaladāna-pratibhuvaṃ) – guarantor of fruit-giving (phala:fruit | dāna:giving | pratibhuvaṃ:guarantor); श्रुतौ (śrutau) – in the Vedas; श्रद्धां (śraddhāṃ) – faith; बध्वा (badhvā) – having bound; दृढपरिकरः (dṛḍhaparikaraḥ) – firmly engaged (dṛḍha:firm | parikaraḥ:engaged); कर्मसु (karmasu) – in actions; जनः (janaḥ) – people;

contentBlocks.original:
क्रियादक्षो दक्षः क्रतुपतिरधीशस्तनुभृतां
ऋषीणामार्त्विज्यं शरणद सदस्याः सुर-गणाः ।
क्रतुभ्रंशस्त्वत्तः क्रतुफल-विधान-व्यसनिनः
ध्रुवं कर्तुं श्रद्धा विधुरमभिचाराय हि मखाः ॥ २१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kriyādakṣo dakṣaḥ kratupatiradhīśastanubhṛtāṃ
ṛṣīṇāmārtvijyaṃ śaraṇada sadasyāḥ sura-gaṇāḥ .
kratubhraṃśastvattaḥ kratuphala-vidhāna-vyasaninaḥ
dhruvaṃ kartuṃ śraddhā vidhuramabhicārāya hi makhāḥ .. 21..
contentBlocks.translation: All the same, O Protector though you exert to reward all sacrifices. Those done without faith in you become counter- productive, as exemplified in the case of the sacrifice performed by Daksha; Daksha was well-versed in the art of sacrifices and himself the Lord of Creation; besides, he was the chief performer: the great maharishis were the priests and the various gods were the participants! (Daksha did not invite Shiva and insulted him greatly; thus enraged, Shiva destroyed the sacrifice and Daksha too). (21)
contentBlocks.meanings: क्रियादक्षः (kriyādakṣaḥ) – skilled in rituals (kriyā:rituals | dakṣaḥ:skilled); दक्षः (dakṣaḥ) – Daksha; क्रतुपतिः (kratupatiḥ) – lord of sacrifices (kratu:sacrifice | patiḥ:lord); अधीशः (adhīśaḥ) – supreme lord; तनुभृताम् (tanubhṛtām) – of embodied beings (tanu:body | bhṛtām:of those who bear); ऋषीणाम् (ṛṣīṇām) – of sages; आर्त्विज्यं (ārtvijyaṃ) – priesthood; शरणद (śaraṇada) – O protector; सदस्याः (sadasyāḥ) – participants; सुर-गणाः (sura-gaṇāḥ) – groups of gods (sura:god | gaṇāḥ:groups); क्रतुभ्रंशः (kratubhraṃśaḥ) – sacrifice destruction (kratu:sacrifice | bhraṃśaḥ:destruction); त्वत्तः (tvattaḥ) – from you; क्रतुफल-विधान-व्यसनिनः (kratuphala-vidhāna-vyasaninaḥ) – obsessed with sacrifice results (kratu:sacrifice | phala:result | vidhāna:arrangement | vyasaninaḥ:obsessed); ध्रुवं (dhruvaṃ) – certainly; कर्तुं (kartuṃ) – to perform; श्रद्धा (śraddhā) – faith; विधुरम् (vidhuram) – lacking; अभिचाराय (abhicārāya) – for counter-productive; हि (hi) – indeed; मखाः (makhāḥ) – sacrifices;

contentBlocks.original:
प्रजानाथं नाथ प्रसभमभिकं स्वां दुहितरं
गतं रोहिद् भूतां रिरमयिषुमृष्यस्य वपुषा ।
धनुष्पाणेर्यातं दिवमपि सपत्राकृतममुं
त्रसन्तं तेऽद्यापि त्यजति न मृगव्याधरभसः ॥ २२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prajānāthaṃ nātha prasabhamabhikaṃ svāṃ duhitaraṃ
gataṃ rohid bhūtāṃ riramayiṣumṛṣyasya vapuṣā .
dhanuṣpāṇeryātaṃ divamapi sapatrākṛtamamuṃ
trasantaṃ te'dyāpi tyajati na mṛgavyādharabhasaḥ .. 22..
contentBlocks.translation: O, Protector! Once Brahma became infatuated with his own daughter. When she fled taking the form of a female deer he also took the form of a male deer and chased her. You took the form of a hunter and went after him, with a bow in hand. Struck by your arrow and very much frightened, Brahma fled to the sky taking the form of a star. Even today he stands frightened by you. (22)
contentBlocks.meanings: प्रजानाथं (prajānāthaṃ) – Protector of beings (prajā:beings | nāthaṃ:protector); नाथ (nātha) – O Lord; प्रसभम् (prasabham) – forcefully; अभिकं (abhikaṃ) – towards; स्वां (svāṃ) – own; दुहितरं (duhitaraṃ) – daughter; गतं (gataṃ) – went; रोहिद् (rohid) – female deer; भूतां (bhūtāṃ) – having become; रिरमयिषुम् (riramayiṣum) – desiring to enjoy; ऋष्यस्य (ṛṣyasya) – of a male deer; वपुषा (vapuṣā) – with the form; धनुष्पाणेः (dhanuṣpāṇeḥ) – with bow in hand (dhanuṣ:bow | pāṇeḥ:in hand); यातं (yātaṃ) – went; दिवम् (divam) – to the sky; अपि (api) – even; सपत्राकृतम् (sapatrākṛtam) – made with wings (sa:with | patra:wings | ākṛtam:made); अमुम् (amum) – him; त्रसन्तं (trasantaṃ) – frightened; ते (te) – by you; अद्यापि (adyāpi) – even today; त्यजति (tyajati) – leaves; न (na) – not; मृगव्याधरभसः (mṛgavyādharabhasaḥ) – hunter's force (mṛga:deer | vyādha:hunter | rabhasaḥ:force);

contentBlocks.original:
स्वलावण्याशंसा धृतधनुषमह्नाय तृणवत्
पुरः प्लुष्टं दृष्ट्वा पुरमथन पुष्पायुधमपि ।
यदि स्त्रैणं देवी यमनिरत-देहार्ध-घटनात्
अवैति त्वामद्धा बत वरद मुग्धा युवतयः ॥ २३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
svalāvaṇyāśaṃsā dhṛtadhanuṣamahnāya tṛṇavat
puraḥ pluṣṭaṃ dṛṣṭvā puramathana puṣpāyudhamapi .
yadi straiṇaṃ devī yamanirata-dehārdha-ghaṭanāt
avaiti tvāmaddhā bata varada mugdhā yuvatayaḥ .. 23..
contentBlocks.translation: O, destroyer of the three cities! Boon- giver! Practitioner of austerities! Before the very eyes of Parvati, you reduced Manmatha (the god of love) to ashes,the moment he tried to arouse passion in you for Parvati, by shooting his famous flower arrows. Even after witnessing this, if Parvati, thinks that you are attracted by her physical charm, on the basis of your sharing half the body with her, certainly women are under self-delusion. (23)
contentBlocks.meanings: स्वलावण्याशंसा (svalāvaṇyāśaṃsā) – desire for own beauty (sva:own | lāvaṇya:beauty | āśaṃsā:desire); धृतधनुषम् (dhṛtadhanuṣam) – holding the bow (dhṛta:holding | dhanuṣam:bow); अह्नाय (ahnāya) – to the day; तृणवत् (tṛṇavat) – like grass; पुरः (puraḥ) – before; प्लुष्टं (pluṣṭam) – burnt; दृष्ट्वा (dṛṣṭvā) – having seen; पुरमथन (puramathana) – destroyer of cities (pura:cities | mathana:destroyer); पुष्पायुधम् (puṣpāyudham) – flower-weaponed (puṣpa:flower | āyudham:weapon); अपि (api) – even; यदि (yadi) – if; स्त्रैणम् (straiṇam) – womanly; देवी (devī) – goddess; यमनिरत (yamanirata) – engaged in control (yama:control | nirata:engaged); देहार्ध (dehārdha) – half of the body (deha:body | ardha:half); घटनात् (ghaṭanāt) – due to union; अवैति (avaiti) – considers; त्वाम् (tvām) – you; अद्धा (addhā) – certainly; बत (bata) – alas; वरद (varada) – boon-giver; मुग्धा (mugdhā) – deluded; युवतयः (yuvatayaḥ) – young women;

contentBlocks.original:
श्मशानेष्वाक्रीडा स्मरहर पिशाचाः सहचराः
चिता-भस्मालेपः स्रगपि नृकरोटी-परिकरः ।
अमङ्गल्यं शीलं तव भवतु नामैवमखिलं
तथापि स्मर्तॄणां वरद परमं मङ्गलमसि ॥ २४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śmaśāneṣvākrīḍā smarahara piśācāḥ sahacarāḥ
citā-bhasmālepaḥ sragapi nṛkaroṭī-parikaraḥ .
amaṅgalyaṃ śīlaṃ tava bhavatu nāmaivamakhilaṃ
tathāpi smartṝṇāṃ varada paramaṃ maṅgalamasi .. 24..
contentBlocks.translation: O, boon giver! O, destroyer of Cupid! You play in the burning ghats. Your friends are the ghosts. Your body is smeared with the ashes of the dead bodies. Your garland is of human skulls. Every aspect of your character is thus inauspicious. Let it be. It does not matter. Because, with all these known oddness, you are quick to grant all auspicious things to the people who just think of you. (It is interesting to note here that in his Devi aparaadha kshamApana stotra Shankaracharya says that,despite his poor and deficient possessions,Shiva got the power to grant boons entirely because because of his having taken the hand of Parvathi in marriage; in the previous shloka, Pushhpadanta calls it naive on the part of Parvati, if she thinks that Shiva is attracted by her charm simply because he is sharing half the body with her.This dichotomy etc. is due to the custom that when a particular lord is to be extolled, the other gods are to be belittled to some extent). (24)
contentBlocks.meanings: श्मशानेषु (śmaśāneṣu) – in the cremation grounds; आक्रीडा (ākrīḍā) – play; स्मरहर (smarahara) – destroyer of Cupid (smara:Cupid | hara:destroyer); पिशाचाः (piśācāḥ) – ghosts; सहचराः (sahacarāḥ) – companions; चिता-भस्मालेपः (citā-bhasmālepaḥ) – smeared with ashes (citā:funeral pyre | bhasma:ashes | ālepaḥ:smeared); स्रगपि (sragapi) – garland also (srag:garland | api:also); नृकरोटी-परिकरः (nṛkaroṭī-parikaraḥ) – adorned with skulls (nṛ:human | karoṭī:skull | parikaraḥ:adorned); अमङ्गल्यम् (amaṅgalyam) – inauspicious; शीलं (śīlaṃ) – character; तव (tava) – your; भवतु (bhavatu) – let it be; नाम (nāma) – indeed; एवम् (evam) – thus; अखिलं (akhilaṃ) – entirely; तथापि (tathāpi) – nevertheless; स्मर्तॄणां (smartṝṇāṃ) – of those who remember; वरद (varada) – boon giver; परमं (paramaṃ) – supreme; मङ्गलम् (maṅgalam) – auspiciousness; असि (asi) – you are;

contentBlocks.original:
मनः प्रत्यक् चित्ते सविधमविधायात्त-मरुतः
प्रहृष्यद्रोमाणः प्रमद-सलिलोत्सङ्गति-दृशः ।
यदालोक्याह्लादं ह्रद इव निमज्यामृतमये
दधत्यन्तस्तत्त्वं किमपि यमिनस्तत् किल भवान् ॥ २५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
manaḥ pratyak citte savidhamavidhāyātta-marutaḥ
prahṛṣyadromāṇaḥ pramada-salilotsaṅgati-dṛśaḥ .
yadālokyāhlādaṃ hrada iva nimajyāmṛtamaye
dadhatyantastattvaṃ kimapi yaminastat kila bhavān .. 25..
contentBlocks.translation: The great yogis regulate their breath, control and still their mind, look inward and enjoy the bliss with their hair standing on edge and eyes filled with tears of joy. It looks as though they are immersed in nectar. That bliss which they see in their heart and exult thus, is verily you Yourself! (25)
contentBlocks.meanings: मनः (manaḥ) – mind; प्रत्यक् (pratyak) – inward; चित्ते (citte) – in the consciousness; सविधम् (savidham) – together; अविधाय (avidhāya) – without placing; आत्त (ātta) – controlled; मरुतः (marutaḥ) – breaths; प्रहृष्यद्रोमाणः (prahṛṣyadromāṇaḥ) – with hair standing on end (prahṛṣyat:thrilled | romāṇaḥ:hair); प्रमद-सलिलोत्सङ्गति-दृशः (pramada-salilotsaṅgati-dṛśaḥ) – eyes filled with tears of joy (pramada:joy | salila:water | utsaṅgati:overflowing | dṛśaḥ:eyes); यदालोक्य (yadālokya) – having seen which; आह्लादं (āhlādam) – bliss; ह्रद (hrada) – lake; इव (iva) – like; निमज्य (nimajya) – immersed; अमृतमये (amṛtamaye) – in nectar (amṛta:nectar | maye:in); दधति (dadhati) – hold; अन्तः (antaḥ) – within; तत्त्वं (tattvam) – truth; किमपि (kimapi) – something; यमिनः (yaminaḥ) – ascetics; तत् (tat) – that; किल (kila) – indeed; भवान् (bhavān) – you;

contentBlocks.original:
त्वमर्कस्त्वं सोमस्त्वमसि पवनस्त्वं हुतवहः
त्वमापस्त्वं व्योम त्वमु धरणिरात्मा त्वमिति च ।
परिच्छिन्नामेवं त्वयि परिणता बिभ्रति गिरं
न विद्मस्तत्तत्त्वं वयमिह तु यत् त्वं न भवसि ॥ २६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvamarkastvaṃ somastvamasi pavanastvaṃ hutavahaḥ
tvamāpastvaṃ vyoma tvamu dharaṇirātmā tvamiti ca .
paricchinnāmevaṃ tvayi pariṇatā bibhrati giraṃ
na vidmastattattvaṃ vayamiha tu yat tvaṃ na bhavasi .. 26..
contentBlocks.translation: You are the sun, the moon, the air, the fire, the water, the sky(ether/space), and the earth (the five elements or ‘bhUtA’s). You are the Self which is omnipresent. Thus people describe in words every attribute as yours. On the other hand, I do not know any fundamental principle or thing or substance, which you are not! (26)
contentBlocks.meanings: त्वम् (tvam) – you; अर्कः (arkaḥ) – sun; त्वम् (tvam) – you; सोमः (somaḥ) – moon; त्वम् (tvam) – you; असि (asi) – are; पवनः (pavanaḥ) – air; त्वम् (tvam) – you; हुतवहः (hutavahaḥ) – fire; त्वम् (tvam) – you; आपः (āpaḥ) – water; त्वम् (tvam) – you; व्योम (vyoma) – sky; त्वम् (tvam) – you; धरणिः (dharaṇiḥ) – earth; आत्मा (ātmā) – self; त्वम् (tvam) – you; इति (iti) – thus; च (ca) – and; परिच्छिन्नाम् (paricchinnām) – limited; एवम् (evam) – thus; त्वयि (tvayi) – in you; परिणता (pariṇatā) – transformed; बिभ्रति (bibhrati) – bear; गिरं (giraṃ) – words; न (na) – not; विद्मः (vidmaḥ) – know; तत्तत्त्वं (tattattvaṃ) – that essence (tat:that | tattvaṃ:essence); वयम् (vayam) – we; इह (iha) – here; तु (tu) – but; यत् (yat) – which; त्वम् (tvam) – you; न (na) – not; भवसि (bhavasi) – are;

contentBlocks.original:
त्रयीं तिस्रो वृत्तीस्त्रिभुवनमथो त्रीनपि सुरान्
अकाराद्यैर्वर्णैस्त्रिभिरभिदधत् तीर्णविकृति ।
तुरीयं ते धाम ध्वनिभिरवरुन्धानमणुभिः
समस्त-व्यस्तं त्वां शरणद गृणात्योमिति पदम् ॥ २७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
trayīṃ tisro vṛttīstribhuvanamatho trīnapi surān
akārādyairvarṇaistribhirabhidadhat tīrṇavikṛti .
turīyaṃ te dhāma dhvanibhiravarundhānamaṇubhiḥ
samasta-vyastaṃ tvāṃ śaraṇada gṛṇātyomiti padam .. 27..
contentBlocks.translation: O, grantor of refuge and protection! The word ‘OM’ consists of the three letters ‘a’, ‘u’ and ‘m’. It refers to the three Vedas (Rik, YajuH and SAma), the three states (Jaagrat.h, Swapna, and sushhupti-awakened, dreaming and sleeping),the three worlds (BhUH, bhuvaH and suvaH) and the three gods (Brahma, VishhNu amd Mahesha). It refers to you yourself both through the individual letters as well as collectively; in the latter form (i.e., the total word ‘OM’) it refers to your omnipresent absolute nature, as the fourth state of existence i.e., ‘turIyaM’ (sleep- like yet awakened and alert state, as a fully- drawn bow). (27)
contentBlocks.meanings: त्रयीं (trayīṃ) – threefold; तिस्रः (tisraḥ) – three; वृत्तिः (vṛttiḥ) – states; त्रिभुवनम् (tribhuvanam) – three worlds (tri:three | bhuvanam:worlds); अथ (atha) – and; त्रीन् (trīn) – three; अपि (api) – also; सुरान् (surān) – gods; अकाराद्यैः (akārādyaiḥ) – beginning with 'a' (akāra:'a' | ādyaiḥ:beginning with); वर्णैः (varṇaiḥ) – letters; त्रिभिः (tribhiḥ) – by three; अभिदधत् (abhidadhat) – expressing; तीर्णविकृति (tīrṇavikṛti) – transcending change (tīrṇa:transcending | vikṛti:change); तुरीयम् (turīyam) – fourth state; ते (te) – your; धाम (dhāma) – abode; ध्वनिभिः (dhvanibhiḥ) – by sounds; अवरुन्धान (avarundhāna) – encompassing; मणुभिः (maṇubhiḥ) – by atoms; समस्त-व्यस्तम् (samasta-vyastam) – entire and distinct (samasta:entire | vyastam:distinct); त्वाम् (tvām) – you; शरणद (śaraṇada) – grantor of refuge (śaraṇa:refuge | da:giver); गृणाति (gṛṇāti) – praises; ओम् (om) – OM; इति (iti) – thus; पदम् (padam) – word;

contentBlocks.original:
भवः शर्वो रुद्रः पशुपतिरथोग्रः सहमहान्
तथा भीमेशानाविति यदभिधानाष्टकमिदम् ।
अमुष्मिन् प्रत्येकं प्रविचरति देव श्रुतिरपि
प्रियायास्मैधाम्ने प्रणिहित-नमस्योऽस्मि भवते ॥ २८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhavaḥ śarvo rudraḥ paśupatirathograḥ sahamahān
tathā bhīmeśānāviti yadabhidhānāṣṭakamidam .
amuṣmin pratyekaṃ pravicarati deva śrutirapi
priyāyāsmaidhāmne praṇihita-namasyo'smi bhavate .. 28..
contentBlocks.translation: I salute you as the dear abode of the following 8 names: bhava, sharva, rudra, pashupati, ugra, sahamahAn.h, bhiima, and Ishaana; the ‘Vedas’ also discusses individually about these names. (28)
contentBlocks.meanings: भवः (bhavaḥ) – Bhava; शर्वः (śarvaḥ) – Sharva; रुद्रः (rudraḥ) – Rudra; पशुपतिः (paśupatiḥ) – Pashupati; अथ (atha) – and; उग्रः (ugraḥ) – Ugra; सहमहान् (sahamahān) – Sahamahān; तथा (tathā) – also; भीमेशानौ (bhīmeśānau) – Bhima and Ishana (bhīma:Bhima | īśāna:Ishana); इति (iti) – thus; यद् (yad) – which; अभिधानाष्टकम् (abhidhānāṣṭakam) – eightfold names (abhidhāna:names | aṣṭakam:eightfold); इदम् (idam) – this; अमुष्मिन् (amuṣmin) – in this; प्रत्येकं (pratyekaṃ) – individually; प्रविचरति (pravicarati) – discusses; देव (deva) – O God; श्रुतिः (śrutiḥ) – Vedas; अपि (api) – also; प्रियाय (priyāya) – dear; अस्मै (asmai) – to this; धाम्ने (dhāmne) – abode; प्रणिहित-नमस्यः (praṇihita-namasyaḥ) – salutation offered (praṇihita:offered | namasyaḥ:salutation); अस्मि (asmi) – I am; भवते (bhavate) – to you;

contentBlocks.original:
नमो नेदिष्ठाय प्रियदव दविष्ठाय च नमः
नमः क्षोदिष्ठाय स्मरहर महिष्ठाय च नमः ।
नमो वर्षिष्ठाय त्रिनयन यविष्ठाय च नमः
नमः सर्वस्मै ते तदिदमतिसर्वाय च नमः ॥ २९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namo nediṣṭhāya priyadava daviṣṭhāya ca namaḥ
namaḥ kṣodiṣṭhāya smarahara mahiṣṭhāya ca namaḥ .
namo varṣiṣṭhāya trinayana yaviṣṭhāya ca namaḥ
namaḥ sarvasmai te tadidamatisarvāya ca namaḥ .. 29..
contentBlocks.translation: O, destroyer of Cupid! O, the three- eyed one! Salutations to you, who is the forest-lover, the nearest and the farthest; the minutest and the biggest, the oldest and the youngest; salutations to you who is everything and beyond everything! (29)
contentBlocks.meanings: नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; नेदिष्ठाय (nediṣṭhāya) – to the nearest; प्रियदव (priyadava) – forest-lover (priya:beloved | dava:forest); दविष्ठाय (daviṣṭhāya) – to the farthest; च (ca) – and; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; क्षोदिष्ठाय (kṣodiṣṭhāya) – to the minutest; स्मरहर (smarahara) – destroyer of Cupid (smara:Cupid | hara:destroyer); महिष्ठाय (mahiṣṭhāya) – to the biggest; च (ca) – and; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; नमो (namo) – salutations; वर्षिष्ठाय (varṣiṣṭhāya) – to the oldest; त्रिनयन (trinayana) – three-eyed one (tri:three | nayana:eye); यविष्ठाय (yaviṣṭhāya) – to the youngest; च (ca) – and; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; सर्वस्मै (sarvasmai) – to everything; ते (te) – to you; तदिदम् (tadidam) – this; अतिसर्वाय (atisarvāya) – beyond everything; च (ca) – and; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations;

contentBlocks.original:
बहुल-रजसे विश्वोत्पत्तौ भवाय नमो नमः
प्रबल-तमसे तत् संहारे हराय नमो नमः ।
जन-सुखकृते सत्त्वोद्रिक्तौ मृडाय नमो नमः
प्रमहसि पदे निस्त्रैगुण्ये शिवाय नमो नमः ॥ ३०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bahula-rajase viśvotpattau bhavāya namo namaḥ
prabala-tamase tat saṃhāre harāya namo namaḥ .
jana-sukhakṛte sattvodriktau mṛḍāya namo namaḥ
pramahasi pade nistraiguṇye śivāya namo namaḥ .. 30..
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to you in the name of ‘Bhava’ in as much as you create the world by taking the ‘rajas’ as the dominant quality; salutations to you in the name of ‘Hara’ in as much as you destroy the world by taking the ‘tamas’ as the dominant quality; salutations to you in the name of ‘MRiDa’, in as much as you maintain and protect the world by taking ‘satva’ as the dominant quality. Again salutations to you in the name of Shiva in as much as you are beyond the above- mentioned three qualities and are the seat of the supreme bliss. (30)
contentBlocks.meanings: बहुल-रजसे (bahula-rajase) – abundant in rajas (bahula:abundant | rajase:in rajas); विश्वोत्पत्तौ (viśvotpattau) – in the creation of the world (viśva:world | utpattau:in creation); भवाय (bhavāya) – to Bhava; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; प्रबल-तमसे (prabala-tamase) – strong in tamas (prabala:strong | tamase:in tamas); तत् (tat) – that; संहारे (saṃhāre) – in destruction; हराय (harāya) – to Hara; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; जन-सुखकृते (jana-sukhakṛte) – for the happiness of people (jana:people | sukhakṛte:for happiness); सत्त्वोद्रिक्तौ (sattvodriktau) – in the abundance of sattva (sattva:sattva | udriktau:in abundance); मृडाय (mṛḍāya) – to Mṛḍa; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; प्रमहसि (pramahasi) – in the supreme; पदे (pade) – in the state; निस्त्रैगुण्ये (nistraiguṇye) – beyond three qualities (nis:beyond | trai:three | guṇye:qualities); शिवाय (śivāya) – to Shiva; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations;

contentBlocks.original:
कृश-परिणति-चेतः क्लेशवश्यं क्व चेदं
क्व च तव गुण-सीमोल्लङ्घिनी शश्वदृद्धिः ।
इति चकितममन्दीकृत्य मां भक्तिराधाद्
वरद चरणयोस्ते वाक्य-पुष्पोपहारम् ॥ ३१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṛśa-pariṇati-cetaḥ kleśavaśyaṃ kva cedaṃ
kva ca tava guṇa-sīmollaṅghinī śaśvadṛddhiḥ .
iti cakitamamandīkṛtya māṃ bhaktirādhād
varada caraṇayoste vākya-puṣpopahāram .. 31..
contentBlocks.translation: O, boon-giver! I was very perplexed to sing your praise considering my little awareness and afflicted mind vis-a-vis your ever increasing limitless quality; however, my devotion to you made me set aside this diffidence and place these floral lines at your feet. (31)
contentBlocks.meanings: कृश (kṛśa) – thin; परिणति (pariṇati) – matured; चेतः (cetaḥ) – mind; क्लेशवश्यम् (kleśavaśyam) – subject to suffering (kleśa:suffering | vaśyam:subject to); क्व (kva) – where; च (ca) – and; इदं (idaṃ) – this; क्व (kva) – where; च (ca) – and; तव (tava) – your; गुण (guṇa) – qualities; सीमोल्लङ्घिनी (sīmollaṅghinī) – transcending limits (sīmā:limit | ullaṅghinī:transcending); शश्वदृद्धिः (śaśvadṛddhiḥ) – ever-increasing prosperity (śaśvat:ever | ṛddhiḥ:prosperity); इति (iti) – thus; चकितम् (cakitam) – fearful; अमन्दीकृत्य (amandīkṛtya) – making not slow (amanda:not slow | kṛtya:making); माम् (mām) – me; भक्तिः (bhaktiḥ) – devotion; आधात् (ādhāt) – placed; वरद (varada) – boon-giver; चरणयोः (caraṇayoḥ) – at the feet; ते (te) – your; वाक्य (vākya) – words; पुष्पोपहारम् (puṣpopahāram) – floral offering (puṣpa:flower | upahāram:offering);

contentBlocks.original:
असित-गिरि-समं स्यात् कज्जलं सिन्धु-पात्रे
सुर-तरुवर-शाखा लेखनी पत्रमुर्वी ।
लिखति यदि गृहीत्वा शारदा सर्वकालं
तदपि तव गुणानामीश पारं न याति ॥ ३२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
asita-giri-samaṃ syāt kajjalaṃ sindhu-pātre
sura-taruvara-śākhā lekhanī patramurvī .
likhati yadi gṛhītvā śāradā sarvakālaṃ
tadapi tava guṇānāmīśa pāraṃ na yāti .. 32..
contentBlocks.translation: O, great master! Even, if one were to assume that the blue mountain, the ocean, the heavenly tree and the earth are the ink,the ink-pot, the pen and the paper respectively and the goddess of learning (Saraswati) herself is the writer,she will not be able to reach the frontiers of your greatness, however long she were to write! (32)
contentBlocks.meanings: असित (asita) – dark; गिरि (giri) – mountain; समं (samaṃ) – equal to; स्यात् (syāt) – may be; कज्जलं (kajjalaṃ) – ink; सिन्धु (sindhu) – ocean; पात्रे (pātre) – in the pot; सुर (sura) – divine; तरुवर (taruvara) – best divine tree (taru:tree | vara:best); शाखा (śākhā) – branch; लेखनी (lekhanī) – pen; पत्रम् (patram) – paper; उर्वी (urvī) – earth; लिखति (likhati) – writes; यदि (yadi) – if; गृहीत्वा (gṛhītvā) – having taken; शारदा (śāradā) – Saraswati; सर्वकालं (sarvakālaṃ) – all the time (sarva:all | kālaṃ:time); तदपि (tadapi) – even then; तव (tava) – your; गुणानाम् (guṇānām) – qualities; ईश (īśa) – O Lord; पारं (pāraṃ) – limit; न (na) – not; याति (yāti) – reaches;

contentBlocks.original:
असुर-सुर-मुनीन्द्रैरर्चितस्येन्दु-मौलेः
ग्रथित-गुणमहिम्नो निर्गुणस्येश्वरस्य ।
सकल-गण-वरिष्ठः पुष्पदन्ताभिधानः
रुचिरमलघुवृत्तैः स्तोत्रमेतच्चकार ॥ ३३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
asura-sura-munīndrairarcitasyendu-mauleḥ
grathita-guṇamahimno nirguṇasyeśvarasya .
sakala-gaṇa-variṣṭhaḥ puṣpadantābhidhānaḥ
ruciramalaghuvṛttaiḥ stotrametaccakāra .. 33..
contentBlocks.translation: The best one among all groups (Gandharva?), Pushhpadanta by name, composed this charming hymn in none too short metres, in praise of the great lord who wears the moon in his head (Shiva), who is worshipped and glorified by all demons, gods and sages and who is beyond all attributes and forms. (33)
contentBlocks.meanings: असुर (asura) – demon; सुर (sura) – god; मुनीन्द्रैः (munīndraiḥ) – by the great sages (muni:sage | indra:great); अर्चितस्य (arcitasya) – worshipped; इन्दु-मौलेः (indu-mauleḥ) – of the moon-crested (indu:moon | mauli:crest); ग्रथित-गुणमहिम्नः (grathita-guṇamahimnaḥ) – of bound virtues and greatness (grathita:bound | guṇa:virtue | mahima:greatness); निर्गुणस्य (nirguṇasya) – of the attributeless; ईश्वरस्य (īśvarasya) – of the lord; सकल-गण-वरिष्ठः (sakala-gaṇa-variṣṭhaḥ) – best among all groups (sakala:all | gaṇa:groups | variṣṭha:best); पुष्पदन्ताभिधानः (puṣpadantābhidhānaḥ) – named Pushpadanta (puṣpadanta:Pushpadanta | abhidhāna:named); रुचिरमलघुवृत्तैः (ruciramalaghuvṛttaiḥ) – with charming, not short metres (rucira:charming | alaghu:not short | vṛttaiḥ:metres); स्तोत्रम् (stotram) – hymn; एतत् (etat) – this; चकार (cakāra) – composed;

contentBlocks.original:
अहरहरनवद्यं धूर्जटेः स्तोत्रमेतत्
पठति परमभक्त्या शुद्ध-चित्तः पुमान् यः ।
स भवति शिवलोके रुद्रतुल्यस्तथाऽत्र
प्रचुरतर-धनायुः पुत्रवान् कीर्तिमांश्च ॥ ३४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aharaharanavadyaṃ dhūrjaṭeḥ stotrametat
paṭhati paramabhaktyā śuddha-cittaḥ pumān yaḥ .
sa bhavati śivaloke rudratulyastathā'tra
pracuratara-dhanāyuḥ putravān kīrtimāṃśca .. 34..
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever reads this faultless hymn of Shiva daily, with pure mind and great devotion, ultimately reaches Shiva’s domain and becomes equal to him; in this world, he is endowed with children, great wealth, long life and fame. (34)
contentBlocks.meanings: अहरहरनवद्यं (aharaharanavadyaṃ) – daily faultless (ahara:daily | anavadyaṃ:faultless); धूर्जटेः (dhūrjaṭeḥ) – of Dhūrjaṭi (Shiva); स्तोत्रमेतत् (stotrametat) – this hymn (stotram:hymn | etat:this); पठति (paṭhati) – reads; परमभक्त्या (paramabhaktyā) – with great devotion (parama:great | bhaktyā:with devotion); शुद्ध-चित्तः (śuddha-cittaḥ) – pure-minded (śuddha:pure | cittaḥ:mind); पुमान् (pumān) – man; यः (yaḥ) – who; स (sa) – he; भवति (bhavati) – becomes; शिवलोके (śivaloke) – in Shiva's domain (śiva:Shiva | loke:in the domain); रुद्रतुल्यः (rudratulyaḥ) – equal to Rudra (rudra:Rudra | tulyaḥ:equal to); तथाऽत्र (tathā'tra) – and here (tathā:and | atra:here); प्रचुरतर-धनायुः (pracuratara-dhanāyuḥ) – abundant wealth and life (pracuratara:abundant | dhana:wealth | āyuḥ:life); पुत्रवान् (putravān) – endowed with children; कीर्तिमांश्च (kīrtimāṃśca) – and famous (kīrtimān:famous | ca:and);

contentBlocks.original:
महेशान्नापरो देवो महिम्नो नापरा स्तुतिः ।
अघोरान्नापरो मन्त्रो नास्ति तत्त्वं गुरोः परम् ॥ ३५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
maheśānnāparo devo mahimno nāparā stutiḥ .
aghorānnāparo mantro nāsti tattvaṃ guroḥ param .. 35..
contentBlocks.translation: There is no God higher than Mahesha; there is no hymn better than this one. There is no ‘mantra’ greater than ‘OM’ and there is no truth or principle beyond one’s teacher/ spiritual guide. (35)
contentBlocks.meanings: महेशात् (maheśāt) – than Mahesha; न (na) – not; अपरो (aparo) – higher; देवः (devaḥ) – God; महिम्नः (mahimnaḥ) – than greatness; न (na) – not; अपरः (aparaḥ) – better; स्तुतिः (stutiḥ) – hymn; अघोरात् (aghorāt) – than Aghora; न (na) – not; अपरो (aparo) – greater; मन्त्रः (mantraḥ) – mantra; नास्ति (nāsti) – there is not; तत्त्वम् (tattvam) – truth; गुरोः (guroḥ) – than teacher's; परम् (param) – beyond;

contentBlocks.original:
दीक्षा दानं तपस्तीर्थं ज्ञानं यागादिकाः क्रियाः ।
महिम्नस्तव पाठस्य कलां नार्हन्ति षोडशीम् ॥ ३६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dīkṣā dānaṃ tapastīrthaṃ jñānaṃ yāgādikāḥ kriyāḥ .
mahimnastava pāṭhasya kalāṃ nārhanti ṣoḍaśīm .. 36..
contentBlocks.translation: Initiation (into spiritual development), charity, penance, pilgrimage, spiritual knowledge and religious acts like sacrifices are not capable of yielding even one-sixteenth of the return that will result from the reading of this hymn. (36)
contentBlocks.meanings: दीक्षा (dīkṣā) – initiation; दानं (dānaṃ) – charity; तपः (tapaḥ) – penance; तीर्थं (tīrthaṃ) – pilgrimage; ज्ञानं (jñānaṃ) – spiritual knowledge; यागादिकाः (yāgādikāḥ) – sacrifices and similar acts (yāga:sacrifice | ādikāḥ:and similar acts); क्रियाः (kriyāḥ) – acts; महिम्नस्तव (mahimnastava) – of the hymn's greatness (mahimnaḥ:of greatness | stava:hymn); पाठस्य (pāṭhasya) – of the reading; कलां (kalāṃ) – one-sixteenth; नार्हन्ति (nārhanti) – do not yield; षोडशीम् (ṣoḍaśīm) – sixteenth part;

contentBlocks.original:
कुसुमदशन-नामा सर्व-गन्धर्व-राजः
शशिधरवर-मौलेर्देवदेवस्य दासः ।
स खलु निज-महिम्नो भ्रष्ट एवास्य रोषात्
स्तवनमिदमकार्षीद् दिव्य-दिव्यं महिम्नः ॥ ३७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kusumadaśana-nāmā sarva-gandharva-rājaḥ
śaśidharavara-maulerdevadevasya dāsaḥ .
sa khalu nija-mahimno bhraṣṭa evāsya roṣāt
stavanamidamakārṣīd divya-divyaṃ mahimnaḥ .. 37..
contentBlocks.translation: Kusumadanta (equivalent of Pushh- padanta) was the king of all Gandharvas and he was a devotee of the Lord of lords, Shiva, who wears the baby moon (with a few digits only) in his head. He fell from his glorious position due to Shiva’s wrath at his misconduct. It was then that the Gandharva composed this hymn which is the most divine. (37)
contentBlocks.meanings: कुसुमदशन-नामा (kusumadaśana-nāmā) – named Kusumadanta (kusuma:flower | daśana:tooth | nāmā:named); सर्व-गन्धर्व-राजः (sarva-gandharva-rājaḥ) – king of all Gandharvas (sarva:all | gandharva:Gandharvas | rājaḥ:king); शशिधरवर-मौलेः (śaśidharavara-mauleḥ) – of the best moon-crested (śaśidhara:moon-crested | vara:best | mauleḥ:of the head); देवदेवस्य (devadevasya) – of the Lord of lords (deva:lord | devasya:of the lord); दासः (dāsaḥ) – servant; स (sa) – he; खलु (khalu) – indeed; निज-महिम्नः (nija-mahimnaḥ) – of his own glory (nija:own | mahimnaḥ:of glory); भ्रष्टः (bhraṣṭaḥ) – fallen; एव (eva) – indeed; अस्य (asya) – his; रोषात् (roṣāt) – due to anger; स्तवनम् (stavanam) – hymn; इदम् (idam) – this; अकार्षीत् (akārṣīt) – composed; दिव्य-दिव्यं (divya-divyaṃ) – most divine (divya:divine | divyaṃ:most divine); महिम्नः (mahimnaḥ) – of glory;

contentBlocks.original:
सुरगुरुमभिपूज्य स्वर्ग-मोक्षैक-हेतुं
पठति यदि मनुष्यः प्राञ्जलिर्नान्य-चेताः ।
व्रजति शिव-समीपं किन्नरैः स्तूयमानः
स्तवनमिदममोघं पुष्पदन्तप्रणीतम् ॥ ३८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
suragurumabhipūjya svarga-mokṣaika-hetuṃ
paṭhati yadi manuṣyaḥ prāñjalirnānya-cetāḥ .
vrajati śiva-samīpaṃ kinnaraiḥ stūyamānaḥ
stavanamidamamoghaṃ puṣpadantapraṇītam .. 38..
contentBlocks.translation: If an aspirant for heaven and liberation, worships Shiva,the teacher of gods, at first and then reads this unfailing hymn, composed by Pushhpadanta, with folded hands and single-mindedness, he attains Shiva’s abode, being praised by ‘kinnaras’ (a group of semi- gods known for their singing talent). (38)
contentBlocks.meanings: सुरगुरुमभिपूज्य (suragurumabhipūjya) – worshipping the teacher of gods (sura:gods | gurum:teacher | abhipūjya:worshipping); स्वर्ग-मोक्षैक-हेतुं (svarga-mokṣaika-hetuṃ) – cause of heaven and liberation (svarga:heaven | mokṣa:liberation | eka:one | hetuṃ:cause); पठति (paṭhati) – reads; यदि (yadi) – if; मनुष्यः (manuṣyaḥ) – a person; प्राञ्जलिः (prāñjaliḥ) – with folded hands; न (na) – not; अन्य-चेताः (anya-cetāḥ) – with single-mindedness (anya:other | cetāḥ:mind); व्रजति (vrajati) – goes; शिव-समीपं (śiva-samīpaṃ) – near Shiva (śiva:Shiva | samīpaṃ:near); किन्नरैः (kinnaraiḥ) – by kinnaras; स्तूयमानः (stūyamānaḥ) – being praised; स्तवनमिदममोघं (stavanamidamamoghaṃ) – this unfailing hymn (stavanam:hymn | idam:this | amoghaṃ:unfailing); पुष्पदन्तप्रणीतम् (puṣpadantapraṇītam) – composed by Pushpadanta (puṣpadanta:Pushpadanta | praṇītam:composed);

contentBlocks.original:
आसमाप्तमिदं स्तोत्रं पुण्यं गन्धर्व-भाषितम् ।
अनौपम्यं मनोहारि सर्वमीश्वरवर्णनम् ॥ ३९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
āsamāptamidaṃ stotraṃ puṇyaṃ gandharva-bhāṣitam .
anaupamyaṃ manohāri sarvamīśvaravarṇanam .. 39..
contentBlocks.translation: Here ends this meritorious,charming and incomparable hymn, uttered by the Gandharva, all in description of the great master. (39)
contentBlocks.meanings: आसमाप्तम् (āsamāptam) – here ends; इदं (idaṃ) – this; स्तोत्रं (stotraṃ) – hymn; पुण्यं (puṇyaṃ) – meritorious; गन्धर्व-भाषितम् (gandharva-bhāṣitam) – uttered by the Gandharva (gandharva:Gandharva | bhāṣitam:uttered); अनौपम्यम् (anaupamyam) – incomparable; मनोहारि (manohāri) – charming; सर्वम् (sarvam) – all; ईश्वरवर्णनम् (īśvaravarṇanam) – description of the great master (īśvara:great master | varṇanam:description);

contentBlocks.original:
इत्येषा वाङ्मयी पूजा श्रीमच्छङ्कर-पादयोः ।
अर्पिता तेन देवेशः प्रीयतां मे सदाशिवः ॥ ४०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ityeṣā vāṅmayī pūjā śrīmacchaṅkara-pādayoḥ .
arpitā tena deveśaḥ prīyatāṃ me sadāśivaḥ .. 40..
contentBlocks.translation: Thus, this worship in the form of words, is dedicated at the feet of Shri Shankara; may the ever-auspicious lord of the gods be pleased with this. (40)
contentBlocks.meanings: इति (iti) – thus; एषा (eṣā) – this; वाङ्मयी (vāṅmayī) – in the form of words (vāk:speech | mayī:consisting of); पूजा (pūjā) – worship; श्रीमच्छङ्कर-पादयोः (śrīmacchaṅkara-pādayoḥ) – at the feet of Shri Shankara (śrīmat:venerable | śaṅkara:Shankara | pādayoḥ:at the feet); अर्पिता (arpitā) – dedicated; तेन (tena) – by this; देवेशः (deveśaḥ) – lord of the gods (deva:god | īśaḥ:lord); प्रीयतां (prīyatām) – be pleased; मे (me) – my; सदाशिवः (sadāśivaḥ) – ever-auspicious (sadā:always | śivaḥ:auspicious);

contentBlocks.original:
तव तत्त्वं न जानामि कीदृशोऽसि महेश्वर ।
यादृशोऽसि महादेव तादृशाय नमो नमः ॥ ४१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tava tattvaṃ na jānāmi kīdṛśo'si maheśvara .
yādṛśo'si mahādeva tādṛśāya namo namaḥ .. 41..
contentBlocks.translation: I do not know the truth of your nature and how you are. O, great God! My Salutations are to that nature of yours of which you really are. (41)
contentBlocks.meanings: तव (tava) – your; तत्त्वं (tattvaṃ) – truth; न (na) – not; जानामि (jānāmi) – I know; कीदृशः (kīdṛśaḥ) – what kind; असि (asi) – you are; महेश्वर (maheśvara) – great God (mahā:great | īśvara:God); यादृशः (yādṛśaḥ) – as you are; असि (asi) – you are; महादेव (mahādeva) – great God (mahā:great | deva:God); तादृशाय (tādṛśāya) – to that nature; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations;

contentBlocks.original:
एककालं द्विकालं वा त्रिकालं यः पठेन्नरः ।
सर्वपाप-विनिर्मुक्तः शिव लोके महीयते ॥ ४२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ekakālaṃ dvikālaṃ vā trikālaṃ yaḥ paṭhennaraḥ .
sarvapāpa-vinirmuktaḥ śiva loke mahīyate .. 42..
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever reads this once, twice or thrice (in a day) revels in the domain of Shiva, bereft of all sins. (42)
contentBlocks.meanings: एककालं (ekakālaṃ) – once (eka:one | kālaṃ:time); द्विकालं (dvikālaṃ) – twice (dvi:two | kālaṃ:time); वा (vā) – or; त्रिकालं (trikālaṃ) – thrice (tri:three | kālaṃ:time); यः (yaḥ) – who; पठेन्नरः (paṭhennaraḥ) – a man reads (paṭhet:reads | naraḥ:man); सर्वपाप-विनिर्मुक्तः (sarvapāpa-vinirmuktaḥ) – freed from all sins (sarva:all | pāpa:sins | vinirmuktaḥ:freed from); शिव (śiva) – Shiva; लोके (loke) – in the world; महीयते (mahīyate) – is honored;

contentBlocks.original:
श्री पुष्पदन्त-मुख-पङ्कज-निर्गतेन
स्तोत्रेण किल्बिष-हरेण हर-प्रियेण ।
कण्ठस्थितेन पठितेन समाहितेन
सुप्रीणितो भवति भूतपतिर्महेशः ॥ ४३॥
इति श्री पुष्पदन्त विरचितं शिवमहिम्नः स्तोत्रं समाप्तम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrī puṣpadanta-mukha-paṅkaja-nirgatena
stotreṇa kilbiṣa-hareṇa hara-priyeṇa .
kaṇṭhasthitena paṭhitena samāhitena
suprīṇito bhavati bhūtapatirmaheśaḥ .. 43..
iti śrī puṣpadanta viracitaṃ śivamahimnaḥ stotraṃ sampurnam ..
contentBlocks.translation: This hymn which is dear to Shiva, has emerged out of the lotus-like mouth of Pushhpadanta and is capable of removing all sins. May the lord of all beings become greatly pleased with anyone who has learnt this by heart and/or reads or recalls this with single-mindedness! (43)
contentBlocks.meanings: श्री (śrī) – auspicious; पुष्पदन्त-मुख-पङ्कज-निर्गतेन (puṣpadanta-mukha-paṅkaja-nirgaten) – emerged from Pushpadanta's lotus-like mouth (puṣpadanta:Pushpadanta | mukha:mouth | paṅkaja:lotus | nirgaten:emerged from); स्तोत्रेण (stotreṇa) – by the hymn; किल्बिष-हरेण (kilbiṣa-hareṇa) – remover of sins (kilbiṣa:sins | hareṇa:remover); हर-प्रियेण (hara-priyeṇa) – dear to Hara (hara:Hara (Shiva) | priyeṇa:dear to); कण्ठस्थितेन (kaṇṭhasthitena) – held in the throat; पठितेन (paṭhitena) – recited; समाहितेन (samāhitena) – with concentration; सुप्रीणितो (suprīṇito) – greatly pleased; भवति (bhavati) – becomes; भूतपतिः (bhūtapatiḥ) – lord of beings; महेशः (maheśaḥ) – Mahesha (Shiva); इति (iti) – thus; श्री (śrī) – auspicious; पुष्पदन्त (puṣpadanta) – Pushpadanta; विरचितं (viracitaṃ) – composed; शिवमहिम्नः (śivamahimnaḥ) – of Shiva's greatness (śiva:Shiva | mahimnaḥ:greatness); स्तोत्रं (stotraṃ) – hymn; समाप्तम् (samāptam) – completed;
